_comment: Cross-binding conformance reference for interp.bilinear (esm-spec §9.2). Each `success` scenario gives positional inputs [table, axis_x, axis_y, x, y] and the expected scalar. Tolerance is 0/0 because the chosen inputs are exactly representable in IEEE-754 binary64 and the spec-pinned evaluation order (two 1-D x-blends followed by one 1-D y-blend, each in the form `a + w*(b - a)`) produces the same Float64 result on any conformant binding. Bindings that selectively use FMA MUST verify their results still match these references; if a binding cannot meet 0/0 on a particular fixture, raise the issue against esm-94w rather than relaxing the fixture silently. Out-of-range queries clamp per axis to the nearest endpoint (extrapolate-flat).

scenarios.name: exact_corner_origin
scenarios.description: (x, y) = (axis_x[1], axis_y[1]) exactly returns table[1][1]; both wx and wy are 0.

scenarios.name: exact_corner_max
scenarios.description: (x, y) = (axis_x[Nx], axis_y[Ny]) exactly returns table[Nx][Ny]; clamps select the last cell, weights are 1; pinned form `t[i] + 1*(t[i+1] - t[i])` returns `t[i+1]`.

scenarios.name: exact_interior_knot_both_axes
scenarios.description: (x, y) lands exactly on the interior knot in both axes (axis_x[2]=1, axis_y[2]=10); cell convention selects `i = 2, j = 2`, both weights are 0; result = table[2][2] = 11.

scenarios.name: exact_x_knot_blend_y
scenarios.description: x lands exactly on interior knot (wx=0), y midway in cell. row_j and row_jp1 reduce to table[2][1] and table[2][2]; result = 10 + 0.5*(11-10) = 10.5.

scenarios.name: blend_x_exact_y_knot
scenarios.description: x midway in cell i=1 (wx=0.5), y exactly on the interior knot axis_y[2]=10. Cell convention selects j=2 (largest with axis_y[j] ≤ y_q), wy = (10-10)/(20-10) = 0. row_j = table[1][2] + 0.5*(table[2][2]-table[1][2]) = 1 + 0.5*(11-1) = 6; row_jp1 = table[1][3] + 0.5*(table[2][3]-table[1][3]) = 2 + 0.5*(12-2) = 7; result = 6 + 0*(7-6) = 6.

scenarios.name: center_of_first_cell
scenarios.description: (x, y) at the center of cell (i=1, j=1); wx = wy = 0.5; row_j = 0 + 0.5*10 = 5; row_jp1 = 1 + 0.5*10 = 6; result = 5 + 0.5*1 = 5.5.

scenarios.name: below_both_clamps_to_corner
scenarios.description: (x, y) below both axes clamps to (axis_x[1], axis_y[1]); result = table[1][1] = 0.

scenarios.name: above_both_clamps_to_corner
scenarios.description: (x, y) above both axes clamps to (axis_x[Nx], axis_y[Ny]); result = table[Nx][Ny] = 22.

scenarios.name: below_x_only_blend_y
scenarios.description: x below axis_x[1] clamps to axis_x[1]; y midway in cell j=1. wx=0; row_j = 0; row_jp1 = 1; result = 0 + 0.5*1 = 0.5.

scenarios.name: above_y_only_blend_x
scenarios.description: y above axis_y[Ny] clamps to axis_y[Ny] (= 20); x midway in cell i=2 (between axis_x[2]=1 and axis_x[3]=2). wy=1; row_j = table[2][3] + 0.5*(table[3][3]-table[2][3]) = 12 + 0.5*10 = 17. With wy=1, result = row_j + 1*(row_jp1 - row_j); but j is selected as Ny-1 = 2 by the cell convention, so row_j is at axis_y[2]=10 and row_jp1 at axis_y[3]=20. Recompute: cell j = 2, wy = (20-10)/(20-10) = 1; row_j = table[2][2] + 0.5*(table[3][2]-table[2][2]) = 11 + 0.5*10 = 16; row_jp1 = table[2][3] + 0.5*(table[3][3]-table[2][3]) = 12 + 0.5*10 = 17; result = 16 + 1*(17-16) = 17.

scenarios.name: nan_x_returns_nan
scenarios.description: NaN x propagates through wx and the x-blends.

scenarios.name: nan_y_returns_nan
scenarios.description: NaN y propagates through wy and the final y-blend.

scenarios.name: minimal_2x2_grid_center
scenarios.description: Minimal-shape 2x2 table. Center of the single cell; wx=wy=0.5. row_j = 0+0.5*2=1; row_jp1 = 1+0.5*2=2; result = 1+0.5*1 = 1.5.

scenarios.name: minimal_2x2_grid_corner
scenarios.description: Minimal-shape 2x2 table; query at the (max, max) corner returns the (max, max) entry.

scenarios.name: non_uniform_axis_x_blend
scenarios.description: Non-uniform axis_x = [0, 2, 3]; axis_y = [0, 1, 2]; query at center of first x-cell (x=1 → wx = 0.5 over span [0,2]) and y=0.5 (wy=0.5 over span [0,1]). row_j = 0 + 0.5*(20-0) = 10; row_jp1 = 1 + 0.5*(21-1) = 11; result = 10 + 0.5*(11-10) = 10.5.

error_scenarios.name: non_monotonic_axis_x
error_scenarios.description: axis_x decreases. Bindings MUST reject at load time with diagnostic code `interp_non_monotonic_axis`.

error_scenarios.name: non_monotonic_axis_y
error_scenarios.description: axis_y decreases. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_non_monotonic_axis`.

error_scenarios.name: equal_adjacent_axis_x
error_scenarios.description: axis_x has duplicate adjacent entries (denominator zero). Bindings MUST reject with `interp_non_monotonic_axis` (interp.bilinear requires strict monotonicity).

error_scenarios.name: axis_x_length_mismatch
error_scenarios.description: table outer length is 3, axis_x has 2. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_axis_length_mismatch`.

error_scenarios.name: axis_y_length_mismatch
error_scenarios.description: Inner row has 3 entries, axis_y has 2. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_axis_length_mismatch`.

error_scenarios.name: ragged_inner_rows
error_scenarios.description: table inner rows have inconsistent lengths. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_axis_length_mismatch` (every row's length must equal len(axis_y)).

error_scenarios.name: nan_in_axis_x
error_scenarios.description: axis_x contains NaN. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_nan_in_axis`.

error_scenarios.name: nan_in_axis_y
error_scenarios.description: axis_y contains NaN. Bindings MUST reject with `interp_nan_in_axis`.

error_scenarios.name: axis_x_too_short
error_scenarios.description: axis_x has only one entry (no x-cell to blend across). Bindings MUST reject with `interp_axis_too_short`.

error_scenarios.name: axis_y_too_short
error_scenarios.description: axis_y has only one entry (no y-cell to blend across). Bindings MUST reject with `interp_axis_too_short`.
